questions.prompt: What does `feat(auth)!: rename getUserProfile to getProfile` signal?

choices.text: Breaking change. The `!` after the scope marks it as backward-incompatible (in Conventional Commits format). Auto-versioning tools see this and trigger a MAJOR version bump (e.g., 2.5.3 → 3.0.0). Pair with a `BREAKING CHANGE:` footer in the body explaining what callers need to update. Consumers reading the changelog know to read carefully before upgrading. The combination of structured types (feat/fix/etc.) + breaking-change indicator means humans don't have to manually pick the next semver; tools compute it from commit history. Hugely reduces the 'we forgot to bump major' bug.

questions.explanation: `!` marks breaking change → triggers major version bump in auto-versioning tools.

questions.prompt: Team uses squash-and-merge. How to keep Conventional Commits format in main history?

choices.text: Trust developers to format commits inside PRs.

Use PR TITLES as Conventional Commits (since the squash-merge defaults to using the PR title as the squash commit message). Add `amannn/action-semantic-pull-request` as a CI check to validate PR titles follow the format — PRs with non-conforming titles fail CI and can't merge. Document the format in PR template. Individual commits inside PRs can be messy (WIP, fixup, etc.); they get collapsed away. Main's history ends up clean and Conventional Commits-conformant despite messy intra-PR commits. Pair with branch protection requiring the title check; release-please can then auto-generate changelogs from the squashed history.

questions.explanation: Use PR titles + CI check that validates title format. Squash-merge uses title as commit message.
